title: 1.1: Explains the behavior when someone submits a detector configuration pointing to internal addresses (e.g., "requests to localhost succeed", "127.0.0.1 is reachable", "private IPs are not blocked", "no SSRF protection for internal addresses")

1.2: Explains the behavior when someone submits a detector configuration pointing to cloud metadata endpoints (e.g., "metadata endpoint is reachable", "cloud credentials could be exposed", "link-local addresses not blocked", "SSRF to cloud metadata works")

1.3: Explains what certificate validation TruffleHog performs for HTTPS verification requests (e.g., "self-signed certificates are rejected", "uses system CA trust store", "standard TLS validation", "x509 errors for untrusted certs")

1.4: Explains whether traffic interception is possible when someone is on the network path (e.g., "interception possible with corporate proxy", "MITM attack feasible", "traffic visible to network intermediaries", "secrets exposed to interceptors")

1.5: Explains how verification works when a detector has multiple matching patterns in the same file (e.g., "generates multiple verification requests", "Cartesian product of matches", "multiplicative behavior", "verifies more than once")

1.6: Explains whether there is a limit on how many verification requests can be triggered (e.g., "a limit exists", "requests are capped", "there is a maximum", "verification count is bounded", "yes, there's a cap")

1.7 : Explains what data gets sent to the verification webhook endpoint (e.g., "JSON payload with matched secrets", "detector name and regex matches", "secrets transmitted in request body", "matched values sent to webhook")

1.8: Explains how TruffleHog handles complex regex patterns (e.g., "linear time complexity", "no catastrophic backtracking", "protected from ReDoS", "completes quickly regardless of pattern complexity")

1.9: Explains that there is a timeout mechanism that limits how long regex/detector execution can run (e.g., "execution times out", "processing is time-limited", "long-running operations are terminated", "default timeout exists")
